spec_match.rationale: Perfect usage of light-dark() inside background-image to swap inline SVG assets dynamically depending on the color scheme.

interactive.rationale: Allows toggling the color-scheme native state (Auto, Forced Light, Forced Dark) with instant card updating.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches parent blurb, although there is a minor technical mismatch in the description of pseudo-elements versus standard elements.

edge_cases.rationale: Demonstrates three distinct types of icon swaps (Sun/Moon theme icon, semantic Check/X state, and stroke color adaption for a Doc icon).

references.rationale: Includes the required ChromeStatus reference link.

design_system.rationale: Clean implementation adhering to design system guidelines, including accessible aria-labels on the icon spans.

openQuestions.title: Correct lede and parent blurb description
openQuestions.detail: Both the parent `index.html` blurb and the concept page lede state that icons are implemented via `content: light-dark(...)` on pseudo-elements. However, the actual demo implements them via `background-image: light-dark(...)` on standard `<span>` elements. The documentation should be corrected to match the actual implementation.

summary: A highly clear and accessible icon swap demo demonstrating the flexibility of light-dark() with background SVGs. The code is solid, but has a minor documentation mismatch regarding whether background-image or content-based pseudo-elements are used.
